area: MSC 46L05, 46L55, 46L80 (+58B34) — Operator algebras: Cuntz/Cuntz-Krieger algebras, permutative representations, KMS states/Bost-Connes-type systems
hole_justification: Searches run (WebSearch, arXiv listings, Springer): "Collatz C*-algebra Cuntz algebra 3x+1"; ""Collatz" "Cuntz" arXiv operator algebra"; ""3x+1" OR "Collatz" Cuntz-Pimsner / graph C*-algebra / crossed product"; "Collatz permutative representation Bratteli-Jorgensen wavelet"; ""Collatz" Bost-Connes / KMS / Thompson group Cuntz"; "Sørensen/Katsura Collatz C*-algebra"; "Collatz free probability von Neumann Cho Jorgensen"; "Collatz groupoid Deaconu-Renault Exel"; arXiv math.OA recent listing; "Neklyudov Collatz Hardy/Bergman". FOUND: exactly one serious C*-line — Takehiko Mori (Chiba), arXiv:2411.08084 (Nov 2024, published Adv. Oper. Theory 2025) and follow-up arXiv:2508.05713 (Aug 2025), which self-describes (correctly, per my searches) as "a first attempt" at Collatz-vs-operator-algebras. Adjacent but weaker: Leventides-Poulios Koopman-operator papers (arXiv:2010.12987, IFAC 2021), Neklyudov's functional-equation/Hardy-space eigenvalue reformulation, Béhani arXiv:2303.03203 (hypercyclicity, math.FA). NOT FOUND: any paper connecting Collatz to Bratteli-Jorgensen/Kawamura/Davidson-Pitts classification of permutative representations (Mori does not cite BJ at all); any KMS/equilibrium-state analysis of a Collatz algebra; any K-theory/ideal-structure analysis; any Bost-Connes-type Collatz semigroup system; nothing by Sorensen, Katsura, or Exel on Collatz. So: the bridge EXISTS in print (the equivalence is proved), but the entire toolbox on the other side is unapplied.
bridge: Let N1 = {n ≡ 1,5 mod 6} (odd non-multiples of 3), N2 = f(N1) = {n ≡ 4,16 mod 18}, X = N1 ∪ N2, and P the first-return map of Collatz T to X (P|N1: n ↦ 3n+1, a bijection N1→N2; P|N2: n ↦ n/2^k, an injection onto X). The inverse branches g1, g2 of P form a branching function system on X (g1(X)=N1, g2(X)=N2, disjoint, covering X), hence S_i δ_n = δ_{g_i(n)} is a PERMUTATIVE representation π of the Cuntz algebra O_2 on ℓ^2(X) — exactly the Bratteli-Jorgensen setting. Mori (Thm 4.3.10) proved: Collatz ⟺ π is irreducible ⟺ δ_1 is cyclic; his Thm 4.2.7 gives the same for the cruder partial-isometry pair T_1δ_n = δ_{3n+1} (n odd), T_2δ_n = δ_{n/2} (n even) on ℓ^2(N). Under Lagarias's conjugacy of T on Z_2 with the 2-shift, π is the atomic representation of O_2 supported on the (countable, shift-quasi-invariant) set of integer points inside the Cantor spectrum; orbit classes of Collatz = tail-equivalence classes of coding words = irreducible summands. Divergent trajectory ↔ aperiodic-word ("infinite-tail") irreducible summand; extra cycle ↔ extra BJ cycle representation P(w); Collatz true ⟺ π ≅ P(12), the BJ irreducible for the primitive word 12 coming from the cycle 1→4→1.
strongest_adjacent_theorems: Bratteli-Jorgensen (Mem. AMS 139 (1999) no. 663; arXiv:funct-an/9612002): complete classification of permutative representations of O_N — canonical decomposition into irreducibles indexed by orbits of the branching system; cycle representations P(w) irreducible iff w primitive; unitary equivalence ⟺ tail equivalence of coding words. Parallel: Davidson-Pitts, 'Invariant subspaces and hyper-reflexivity for free semigroup algebras', Proc. LMS 78 (1999) 401-430 (classification of atomic representations); Kawamura's generalized permutative representations (arXiv:math/0505101).

Cuntz, 'Simple C*-algebras generated by isometries', Comm. Math. Phys. 57 (1977): O_2 is simple, so EVERY representation is faithful and every family (S_1,S_2) satisfying the relations generates an isomorphic copy — hence K-theory, ideal structure, traces, and any algebra-level invariant are PROVABLY blind to Collatz; all information sits in the spatial/unitary-equivalence data. This is a rigorous no-go for the naive 'K-theory obstruction' hope.

Laca-Neshveyev, 'KMS states of quasi-free dynamics on Pimsner algebras', J. Funct. Anal. 211 (2004) 457-482: KMS_β states of the (non-simple!) Toeplitz-Pimsner extension ⟺ subinvariant traces on the coefficient algebra — for the Collatz correspondence over c_0(N): weights μ with μ(n) ≥ e^{-β}Σ_{m∈T^{-1}(n)}μ(m); critical β = log(growth rate of the backward Collatz tree), which Applegate-Lagarias (Math. Comp. 64 (1995), density bounds I-II) bounded rigorously.

Bost-Connes (Selecta Math. 1 (1995)) and Laca-Raeburn ('Phase transition on the Toeplitz algebra of the affine semigroup over N', Adv. Math. 225 (2010)): complete KMS phase diagrams for number-theoretic semigroup C*-algebras with low-temperature extremal states indexed by arithmetic data — the exact template for the Toeplitz algebra of the free semigroup ⟨x↦2x, x↦3x+1⟩ acting on Z (freeness of that semigroup is known), which no one has written down.

Matsumoto-Matui (Kyoto J. Math. 54 (2014) 863-877): one-sided topological Markov shifts are continuously orbit equivalent iff their Cuntz-Krieger algebras are diagonal-preservingly isomorphic — the rigidity dictionary through which Lagarias's 2-adic conjugacy becomes an operator-algebra statement.
the_push: Pushing the analogy through BJ classification yields, in order: (1) A FREE UPGRADE OF MORI. Since the parity/coding-word map is injective on Z_2 (Lagarias 1985), distinct Collatz orbit classes have non-tail-equivalent coding words, so by BJ/Davidson-Pitts the representation π is multiplicity-free and its commutant is the atomic abelian algebra ℓ^∞(orbit classes). Hence the lattice of reducing subspaces is EXACTLY the power set of the set of Collatz orbit classes, and 'Collatz ⟺ π ≅ P(12)' is the sharpest representation-theoretic statement equivalent to the conjecture (sharper than Mori's irreducibility criterion: it names the representation). Mori proved the equivalence with bare hands and never identified which irreducible it should be, nor the commutant structure. (2) AN HONEST NO-GO: simplicity of O_2 + faithfulness of all its reps proves that K-theory, ideal structure, and algebra-level KMS carry zero information; BJ says the unitary-equivalence data is a loss-free mirror of the orbit partition. So the bridge is exact but tautological at the qualitative level — the classification theory's real contribution is to certify which operator-algebraic invariants CANNOT work, killing several tempting dead ends in advance. (3) THE SURVIVING HANDLES, where genuine new mathematics is available: (a) reducing projections = bounded 0-1 harmonic functions of the backward branching; a spectral-gap/transfer-operator estimate on filtered subspaces gives QUANTITATIVE irreducibility statements ('no invariant set of automaticity ≤ s'), which is precisely the operator-side generalization of this program's just-proved 7-state-DFA no-go — but in the parity-word coding, where T literally IS the 2-shift, defining a NEW certificate class (sofic parity-word divergence certificates) not covered by the base-2 DFA exhaustion, and not covered by Monks 2006 (semilinear = unary-regular sets); (b) KMS weights on the non-simple Toeplitz extension are subinvariant measures for the transfer operator, so the KMS simplex at inverse temperature β encodes backward-tree growth — Laca-Neshveyev + Applegate-Lagarias should give a phase-transition theorem whose critical temperature is the tree growth constant, with any divergent orbit spawning its own extremal KMS ray; best realistic outcome there is a re-derivation (possibly mild sharpening) of Krasikov-Lagarias-type density exponents in Ruelle-operator language, not a proof of the conjecture.
first_move: Lemma 1 (one-to-two weeks, Lean-friendly, publishable as a note): 'Mori's representation π of O_2 on ℓ^2(N1∪N2) is multiplicity-free; its commutant is the atomic abelian von Neumann algebra spanned by orbit-class projections; consequently the Collatz conjecture holds iff π is unitarily equivalent to the Bratteli-Jorgensen cycle representation P(12).' Proof ingredients: injectivity of parity vectors (Lagarias 1985, already in this program's toolkit) + BJ Mem. AMS classification + Davidson-Pitts atomic-representation uniqueness — all citable, the glue is elementary. Then the computation matched to our 32-core machines: define 'sofic parity-word divergence certificates' — an s-state DFA over the return-word alphabet {1,2} whose accepted ω-language contains the coding word of every element of a nonempty P-invariant set avoiding the cycle {1,4} — write the consistency decision procedure (mirroring the existing regular-certificate checker, but in the coordinates where T is conjugate to the 2-shift), and exhaustively refute all certificates with s ≤ 8 states, extending the program's ≤7-state base-2 theorem to a provably different certificate class.
payoff_odds: Realistic best case: (i) a clean classification note upgrading Mori's equivalence to 'Collatz ⟺ π ≅ P(12), commutant = ℓ^∞(orbit classes)' — near-certain to work, modest but real value, and it imports 25 years of permutative-representation machinery into the Collatz literature for the first time; (ii) a machine-checked no-go theorem for the new sofic parity-word certificate class (maybe 20-30% chance this reveals structure the base-2 search could not see, since the coding is exact for the 2-adic conjugacy); (iii) a KMS phase-transition theorem for the Collatz Toeplitz algebra with critical temperature = tree growth constant — publishable operator algebra, but its number-theoretic content would likely reproduce known density bounds rather than exceed them. Likely case: the dictionary is confirmed loss-free-but-tautological; the area's chief gift to this program is negative guidance (provably no K-theoretic or ideal-theoretic obstruction exists — do not spend effort there) plus one new searchable certificate hierarchy. Probability of a genuinely new partial result on Collatz itself: low-to-moderate; of major progress: negligible. Fringe with one active author (Mori) means priority is cheap now and the BJ/KMS holes are real, but the honest reading of the Béhani/Neklyudov line is a cautionary tale — several 'operator reformulations' turn out to have properties (hypercyclicity, chaos) that hold independently of whether Collatz is true.

prior_art.ref: T. Mori, 'Application of Operator Theory for the Collatz Conjecture', Adv. Oper. Theory 10:39 (2025); arXiv:2411.08084 (v2 Feb 2025)
prior_art.what: THE paper on this bridge; self-described first attempt, and my searches confirm it. Three formulations: (1) single operator Tδ_n = δ_{f(n)} on ℓ^2(N) — irreducibility of C*(T) only IMPLIES Collatz (Cor. 4.1.3); (2) partial isometries T_1 (odd branch), T_2 (even branch) — irreducibility of C*(T_1,T_2) EQUIVALENT to Collatz (Thm 4.2.7); (3) via first-return map P on N1∪N2 (N1={1,5 mod 6}, N2={4,16 mod 18}), genuine isometries S_1=T_1*T_2*, S_2=T_2* with S_1S_1*+S_2S_2*=I generating an O_2 copy — irreducibility EQUIVALENT to Collatz (Thm 4.3.10). All proofs elementary (minimality-of-n arguments on matrix entries of commutant projections). STOPS AT: no Bratteli-Jorgensen (not cited), no identification of which irreducible the rep should be, no commutant/multiplicity structure, no KMS, no K-theory, nothing quantitative.

prior_art.ref: T. Mori, 'Dynamical Systems with Bounded Condition and C*-algebras', arXiv:2508.05713 (Aug 2025)
prior_art.what: Abstract framework: bounded/separating/uniqueness conditions on maps of countable sets; order-preserving injection {f-invariant sets} → {reducing subspaces}, bijective under 'totally uniqueness'; irreducibility ⟺ minimality; applies to all qx+d maps; relates the construction to Cuntz-Krieger relations and symbolic dynamics. STOPS AT: the lattice correspondence — no classification of the representation, no invariants, no growth/entropy/KMS.

prior_art.ref: J. Leventides, C. Poulios, 'Koopman operators and the 3x+1-dynamical system', arXiv:2010.12987 (SIAM-adjacent; also IFAC-PapersOnLine 2021 'An operator theoretic approach...')
prior_art.what: Koopman/composition-operator lift of Collatz to ℓ^1/ℓ^2 and a C*-algebra generated by a single isometry (Coburn/Toeplitz setting); Fourier analysis of trajectory sign sequences; reformulations but no equivalence with an operator-algebraic invariant, no Cuntz-algebra structure, nothing on representations. Cited by Mori as the predecessor.

prior_art.ref: V. Béhani, 'Linear dynamics of an operator associated to the Collatz map', arXiv:2303.03203 (math.FA, 2023), building on M. Neklyudov, 'Functional Analysis Approach to the Collatz Conjecture'
prior_art.what: Weighted-Bergman-space operator associated to Collatz shown hypercyclic, ergodic w.r.t. a full-support Gaussian measure, chaotic — INDEPENDENTLY of whether Collatz holds. Neklyudov had reformulated Collatz as an eigenvalue-multiplicity statement on Hardy space. Cautionary precedent: coarse operator properties of Collatz lifts often decouple from the conjecture.

prior_art.ref: O. Bratteli, P.E.T. Jorgensen, 'Iterated function systems and permutation representations of the Cuntz algebra', Mem. AMS 139 (1999) no. 663; arXiv:funct-an/9612002
prior_art.what: The other side of the bridge (no Collatz content): full decomposition/classification theory for permutative representations of O_N — orbits of branching systems ↔ irreducible summands, cycle words ↔ cycle reps P(w), tail equivalence ↔ unitary equivalence. Never yet applied to Mori's representation; this unapplied overlap is the hole.
